extraction.one_line_summary: BMC won the TTT in 22:23, beating Mitchelton-Scott by 4 seconds with Dennis driving the final surge.
tactical_patterns: BMC gained 4 seconds in final 500m with Dennis pulling, suggesting late-stage power reserves decisive over distributed pacing.
Mitchelton-Scott unexpectedly matched Sky/BMC power despite lacking traditional TTT pedigree, finishing only 4 seconds off winning time.
Sky lost TTT despite Froome/Thomas leadership by placing only third at 9 seconds, revealing 2018 depth weakness compared to rivals.
Cavendish mechanical forced road bike substitution mid-TTT, Dimension Data finished 40+ seconds down, eliminating sprint GC protection strategy.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: designated lead-out role but Sky only third at +9 seconds suggests team strength deficit not individual form issue
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: pulled BMC through final meters to overtake Mitchelton-Scott by 4 seconds, demonstrating decisive late-effort capacity
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: crashed during TTT with facial injuries and torn kit, compromised sprint preparation and lost 40+ seconds on GC contenders
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: UAE finished respectable 23:04 matching EF times, suggesting adequate form without revealing GC ceiling
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Sunweb maintained five riders to line including Kelderman, preserving GC position while rivals hemorrhaged time
